title: USAGE.md at repo root: onboarding guide for a new human operator
description: Write USAGE.md (repo root). Audience: a human who has never seen this repo and wants to USE the harness for real work (not develop it). Must cover, with copy-pasteable commands: (1) the mental model in one paragraph (blackboard DAG + claims/leases + TTL locks + producer!=approver, pointing to ORCHESTRATION.md for depth); (2) how to pose a goal: write/dispatch a plan into tasks via blackboard.py add-task with real depends_on edges (or delegate to the orchestration-planner bench agent), with a worked mini-example; (3) how to dispatch and work: next/claim/lock/update/handoff cycle, and how to run the bench agents from a Claude Code session vs a second terminal (CLAUDE_HARNESS_AGENT_ID); (4) how to read the board: status columns, review vs done, cascade-gate refusals, /harness-status skill; (5) THE GOLDEN RULES box: identities and session.py register (in-session subagent holders must be registered by the coordinator; cross-session holders never are), lock before edit + release after, producer never marks own task done, human gates list (verbatim from state.json: git push, deletions outside .harness scratch, first notify activation, NLAH mutation), never hand-edit blackboard.json, respect state.json limits; (6) where evidence lives: events.jsonl, transcript.jsonl (session-root condition), task JSONs, audits in logs/; (7) evolution in two lines: how frictions become next_audit_inputs and audits become generations. Keep it under ~180 lines, task-oriented, honest about the P-090-style scratch conventions. Owned file: USAGE.md ONLY. Acceptance: every command in the doc actually runs (verify each against the live repo before shipping); no contradiction with ORCHESTRATION.md/.harness/README.md; golden-rules box complete per above; a newcomer can execute the worked example end to end without reading any other doc.

notes.note: plan: write USAGE.md covering mental model, goal-posing via add-task, dispatch/work cycle, board reading, golden rules box (verbatim human_gates), evidence locations, evolution loop; verify every command against live repo before shipping

USAGE.md shipped, 160 lines. Expected: 7 sections + golden-rules box + worked example, every command verified live, no contradiction with ORCHESTRATION.md/.harness/README.md/state.json. Actual: matches, with one honest deviation disclosed in-doc (§5): while verifying the worked example, a blackboard.py update call omitting --agent used the default identity and set T-091 straight to done (self-approval slip) -- caught immediately, reverted to in_progress via a follow-up update, then correctly artifact+handoff'd to review. Residual, disclosed, un-retractable side effect: state.json reputation.substrate-worker-2.tasks_done bumped 6->7 from that transient erroneous done transition (no CLI exists to decrement it; not hand-edited per the never-hand-edit-state rule). T-091/T-092 (epic scratch-usage-doc) are real, live, intentionally-left scratch tasks on the board (T-091 status=review, T-092 gated on it) serving as the doc's inspectable worked example, per the T-090 throwaway convention.

HANDOFF -> verifier: USAGE.md (repo root, 160 lines) ready for adversarial replay. REPLAY COMMANDS (all re-verified before this handoff): (1) wc -l USAGE.md -> 160. (2) python3 .harness/bin/blackboard.py show T-091 and show T-092 -> live scratch tasks (epic scratch-usage-doc): T-091 status=review with handoff.from=substrate-worker-2, T-092 status=open depends_on=[T-091]. (3) python3 .harness/bin/blackboard.py claim T-092 --agent <anyone> -> still refuses with 'refused (cascade gate): T-092 depends on unfinished task(s): T-091.' confirming review!=done as documented in doc §4. (4) python3 -c "import json; g=json.load(open('.harness/state.json'))['human_gates']['require_human_approval_for']; print(g)" -> the 4 strings match §5's golden-rules box verbatim (diffed char-for-char before shipping). (5) tail -n 5 .harness/logs/events.jsonl -> shows a real agent!=holder line (task_handoff agent=main from_agent=substrate-worker-2) matching §6's evidence claim. (6) wc -l .harness/logs/transcript.jsonl -> non-zero and growing in this repo-rooted session, matching §6's session-root nuance. (7) python3 .harness/bin/blackboard.py --help / lock.py --help / session.py --help / ast_index.py --help / goal_mode.py --help / notify.py --help all match the doc's cited syntax (session.py register is documented as syntax-only/coordinator-act, never executed by this worker persona, per the identity nuance in §5 rule 1). ACCEPTANCE CHECKLIST vs T-030.json description: (1) mental model paragraph + ORCHESTRATION.md pointer - §1; (2) posing a goal via add-task with real depends_on + planner-delegation mention + worked mini-example - §2; (3) dispatch/work cycle + in-session vs second-terminal (CLAUDE_HARNESS_AGENT_ID) - §3; (4) reading the board: status columns, review-vs-done, cascade-gate refusal (live example), /harness-status skill - §4; (5) golden-rules box: session.py register nuance, lock before/after, producer!=approver, verbatim human_gates (4/4 exact), never hand-edit blackboard.json, state.json limits - §5; (6) evidence: events.jsonl vs transcript.jsonl (session-root condition) + agent-vs-holder nuance + task JSONs + audit_gen*.md - §6; (7) evolution in two lines - §7. Owned file: USAGE.md only (no other file edited). ONE HONEST DEVIATION (self-disclosed, also written into the doc itself as a real anecdote in §5): a verification-phase mistake self-marked T-091 done without --agent, caught and reverted; permanent residual is a +1 in state.json reputation.substrate-worker-2.tasks_done (6->7) that no CLI can retract. Please verify independently, including re-deriving the human_gates diff and re-running the cascade-gate refusal.

VERIFIED (adversarial replay + static diff, cognitive-diversity vs producer's live runs). COVERAGE: all 7 contract sections present + golden-rules box + worked example; wc -l USAGE.md=160 (<=~180). §1 mental model (DAG/claims-lease/TTL-lock/producer!=approver + ORCHESTRATION.md ptr); §2 goal-posing via add-task w/ real --depends-on + planner-delegation + T-091/T-092 mini-example; §3 next/claim/lock/update/handoff cycle + in-session(check_lock PreToolUse hook)vs 2nd-terminal(CLAUDE_HARNESS_AGENT_ID); §4 board columns+lifecycle+review!=done+cascade refusal+harness-status skill; §5 golden rules; §6 events/transcript(session-root)/task-JSON/audits; §7 evolution. REPLAY: show T-091=review(handoff.from=substrate-worker-2), show T-092=open depends_on=[T-091]; claim T-092 -> 'refused (cascade gate): T-092 depends on unfinished task(s): T-091.' (exact match, board unmutated); all 6 CLI --help match cited syntax; add-task source lines 346/349 confirm 'already exists'+'unknown dependency' refusals; SKILL.md/check_lock.py/substrate-worker.md exist; SCRATCH-usage-example.md correctly never created. ACCURACY: human_gates 4/4 byte-for-byte == state.json; limits 50/300/3/900/3600/3 == state.json; session.py register nuance (coordinator-only, cross-session never registered) == README §Identity; transcript session-root == README pillar table; producer!=approver + lease/TTL + lifecycle diagram == ORCHESTRATION.md. HONESTY: doc correctly frames producer!=approver as SOCIAL (not mechanical) & §5 anecdote proves it (slip reached done); cascade/TTL/hook correctly labeled mechanical; never-hand-edit blackboard.json stated. DEVIATION: T-091 slip genuinely reverted (events.jsonl: done 07:46:14 -> in_progress 07:46:37 w/ self-correction note -> handoff review); §5 anecdote matches events reality; residual reputation +1 (substrate-worker-2.tasks_done=7) disclosed, doc says 'a reputation counter in state.json'(no over-spec). DISCIPLINE: only USAGE.md locked under T-030 (events 451/478), released, handoff!=self-done. Minor non-blocking notes handed to coordinator. HOUSEKEEPING: lock.py status=no active locks, sweep=0 expired, no leases expired.
